response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior web/full-stack engineer, but is a poor fit for this embedded security role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency, deep C on UNIX, Linux kernel analysis and modification, and networking security expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3 switching) — none of which are evidenced. C/C++ experience is limited to a brief 2014-2015 stint, and there is no demonstrated security or network-switch background. Language skills listed are English, Romanian, and German, with no Japanese.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling) and open-source contributions
Experience with low-latency, high-throughput systems (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby) and Linux environments
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency, a hard requirement for the role
Minimal recent C programming and no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience
No demonstrated experience with security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, encryption) or L2/L3 switch development

key_factors.factor: Lack of embedded C / Linux kernel / network security experience
Solid general engineering seniority and testing discipline
